sn35.28:1.1: At one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Gayā, on Gayā Head, together with a thousand *bhikkhus*.
sn35.28:1.10: ‘Burning with the fire of lust, with the fire of hatred, with the fire of delusion; burning with birth, aging, and death, with sorrows, lamentations, *dukkhas*, distresses, and despairs,’ I say …
sn35.28:1.11: the mind is burning; *dhammas* are burning; mind-*viññāṇa* [*manoviññāṇaṁ*] is burning; mind-contact is burning. And whatever felt experience arises dependent on mind-contact — whether *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither *dukkha* nor *sukha* — that too is burning.
sn35.28:1.13: ‘Burning with the fire of lust, with the fire of hatred, with the fire of delusion; burning with birth, aging, and death, with sorrows, lamentations, *dukkhas*, distresses, and despairs,’ I say.
sn35.28:1.14: Seeing in this way, *bhikkhus*, the learned noble disciple becomes disenchanted with the eye, becomes disenchanted with forms [*rūpesu*], becomes disenchanted with eye-*viññāṇa* [*cakkhuviññāṇe*], becomes disenchanted with eye-contact, and becomes disenchanted with whatever felt experience arises dependent on eye-contact — whether *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither *dukkha* nor *sukha* …
sn35.28:1.15: and becomes disenchanted with whatever felt experience arises dependent on mind-contact — whether *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither *dukkha* nor *sukha*.
sn35.28:1.16: Being disenchanted, he becomes dispassionate; through dispassion, he is liberated; when liberated, there is the knowledge: ‘liberated.’
sn35.28:1.17: He understands: ‘Birth is destroyed; the holy life has been lived; what had to be done has been done; there is no more of this state of being.’”
sn35.28:1.5: The eye, *bhikkhus*, is burning; forms [*rūpā*] are burning; eye-*viññāṇa* [*cakkhuviññāṇaṁ*] is burning; eye-contact is burning. And whatever felt experience arises dependent on eye-contact — whether *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither *dukkha* nor *sukha* — that too is burning.
sn35.28:1.7: ‘Burning with the fire of lust, with the fire of hatred, with the fire of delusion; burning with birth, aging, and death, with sorrows, lamentations, *dukkhas*, distresses, and despairs,’ I say …
sn35.28:1.8: the tongue is burning; tastes are burning; tongue-*viññāṇa* [*jivhāviññāṇaṁ*] is burning; tongue-contact is burning. And whatever felt experience arises dependent on tongue-contact — whether *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither *dukkha* nor *sukha* — that too is burning.
sn35.28:2.2: Satisfied, those *bhikkhus* rejoiced in the *Bhagavā*’s statement.
sn35.28:2.3: And while this explanation was being spoken, the minds [*cittāni*] of that thousand *bhikkhus* were liberated from the *āsavas* through non-*upādāna* [*anupādāya*].
